title: Being Frank
author: Donna W. Earnhardt
description: A story about a boy named Frank who learns that while honesty is the best policy, it's best served with kindness.

sentences.englishOriginal: Welcome to Storyline Online, brought to you by the SAG-AFTRA Foundation.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 歡迎來到 Storyline Online，本節目由美國演員工會基金會為您呈現。

phraseNotes.title: brought to you by
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用於節目、活動或廣播中，表示「由……贊助、提供或呈現」。
examples: This program is brought to you by our local sponsors.
Tonight's concert is brought to you by the city council.

sentences.englishOriginal: I’m Bob Odenkirk, and today I’m gonna read Being Frank.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我是鮑勃·奧登科克，今天我要朗讀《誠實的法蘭克》（Being Frank）。

sentences.englishOriginal: Written by Donna W. Earnhardt and illustrated by Andrea Castellani.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 本書由唐娜·W·恩哈特著，安德烈亞·卡斯特拉尼繪製插圖。

grammarNotes.title: Past Participle (Reduced Relative Clause)
grammarNotes.explanation: 這是一個省略了主詞與助動詞的過去分詞片語（原為 Which was written by...），在書本封面或版權頁中極為常見，用來精簡地表示「由誰所著/所畫」。
examples: This is a novel written by a famous Japanese author.
The poster, designed by a local artist, won first prize.

sentences.englishOriginal: Frank was always frank.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 法蘭克總是直言不諱。

vocabularyNotes.title: frank
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，意為「坦白的、直率的、直言不諱的」。這裡作者利用了主角的名字「Frank」與形容詞「frank」同音同字的雙關語。
examples: To be frank with you, I don't think this is a good idea.
She gave a frank assessment of the company's financial situation.

sentences.englishOriginal: “Honesty is the best policy,” he said.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「誠實乃上策，」他說道。

vocabularyNotes.title: policy
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，在一般商務中指「政策」或「保單」，但在經典諺語「Honesty is the best policy」中，引申指處事或對待他人的「最佳方針、準則」。
examples: It is company policy to respond to all customer emails within 24 hours.
The school has a strict no-smoking policy.

sentences.englishOriginal: Frank never lied to his schoolmates.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 法蘭克從不對他的同學們說謊。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Your freckles remind me of the Big Dipper,” he told Dotty.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「妳臉上的雀斑讓我想到了北斗七星，」他對多蒂說。

vocabularyNotes.title: freckles
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞（通常用複數），指皮膚上的「雀斑、斑點」。
examples: She has pale skin and a few freckles on her nose.
His face was covered in freckles after a summer at the beach.

phraseNotes.title: remind someone of someone/something
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意為「使某人想起、讓某人聯想到某人或某事物」。
examples: This song always reminds me of my high school days.
You remind me so much of your father when he was young.

sentences.englishOriginal: Dotty pulled her hood over her face.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 多蒂將外套的兜帽拉了下來，蓋住自己的臉。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Your singing is kind of shrieky,” Frank told Carol.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「妳唱歌的聲音聽起來有點尖銳刺耳，」法蘭克對卡洛說。

vocabularyNotes.title: shrieky
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，源自動詞 shriek（尖叫），形容聲音「尖銳的、刺耳的」。
examples: The children were making shrieky noises in the playground.
I heard a shrieky laugh from the next room.

sentences.englishOriginal: Carol stomped away.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 卡洛氣沖沖地跺著腳走開了。

vocabularyNotes.title: stomp
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「重踩、跺腳」；stomp away 則形容一個人因為生氣或挫折而大步跺腳離去。
examples: The angry customer stomped out of the store.
Stop stomping your feet on the floor!

sentences.englishOriginal: Frank always told the truth to adults.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 法蘭克對大人也總是實話實說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Your breath smells funny,” he told his teacher, Ms. Zaroma.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「老師，妳嘴巴的味道聞起來怪怪的，」他對他的老師薩羅馬小姐說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: smells funny
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 道地的口語表達。「funny」在這裡不是指「好笑的」，而是指「奇怪的、不對勁的、有異味的」。
examples: The milk smells funny; I think it has gone bad.
My car is making a funny noise today.

sentences.englishOriginal: She sent Frank to the principal’s office.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 她把法蘭克送進了校長室。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Your toupee looks like my pet weasel,” he told Principal Wiggins.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「校長，您的假髮看起來就像我養的寵物鼬鼠，」他對威金斯校長說。

vocabularyNotes.title: toupee
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，特指男士用的「局部假髮」（通常用來遮蓋禿頭）。
examples: It was obvious that the man was wearing a toupee.
The strong wind blew his toupee right off his head.

sentences.englishOriginal: Mr. Wiggins called Frank’s mom.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 威金斯先生打電話給法蘭克的媽媽。

sentences.englishOriginal: “You wouldn’t get so many wrinkles if you didn’t glare at me like that,” Frank told his mom on the way home.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「如果您不要那樣瞪著我，您就不會長那麼多皺紋了，」法蘭克在回家的路上對他媽媽說。

vocabularyNotes.title: glare
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「怒目而視、狠狠地瞪著」。後續常接介系詞 at。
examples: She glared at him in anger after he broke her favorite vase.
Instead of answering, he just sat there and glared.

phraseNotes.title: on the way home
phraseNotes.explanation: 固定片語，表示「在回家的路上」。這裡 home 是副詞，前面不需要加 to。
examples: I picked up some groceries on the way home.
We can discuss this plan on the way home.

grammarNotes.title: Conditional Sentences (Second Conditional)
grammarNotes.explanation: 與現在事實相反的虛擬語氣。結構為「If + 主詞 + 過去式動詞，主詞 + would/could/might + 原形動詞」。這裡法蘭克的媽媽正在狠狠瞪著他，所以法蘭克用虛擬語氣開玩笑說，如果妳「不瞪我（與現在事實相反）」，就不會有那麼多皺紋。
examples: If I had enough money, I would buy a house right now.
You wouldn't be so tired if you went to bed earlier.

sentences.englishOriginal: “And by the way – you’re speeding.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「而且順帶一提——您超速了。」

phraseNotes.title: by the way
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用口語轉折詞，用於談話中想要「順便一提」或轉移話題時。
examples: By the way, have you seen my keys anywhere?
I enjoyed the movie. By the way, thanks for buying the tickets.

sentences.englishOriginal: And Frank was always, always honest with police officers.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而且法蘭克對警察也總是、總是老老實實。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Yes, officer,” he said. “She knew how fast she was going. I told her.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「是的，警官，」他說。「她知道自己開得多快。我剛剛有告訴她。」

sentences.englishOriginal: Frank was very proud of being frank.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 法蘭克為自己的直言不諱感到非常自豪。

phraseNotes.title: proud of
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意為「以……為榮、為……感到自豪」，後面接名詞或動名詞。
examples: Her parents are very proud of her academic achievements.
You should be proud of yourself for working so hard.

sentences.englishOriginal: But other than police officers, adults weren’t as impressed with Frank’s honesty as he thought they should be.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但除了警察之外，大人們對法蘭克誠實的態度，並沒有像他預期中的那樣讚賞。

phraseNotes.title: other than
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意為「除了……之外」。
examples: Are there any open tables other than the ones outside?
I don't know anyone here other than Susan.

phraseNotes.title: impressed with
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，表示「對……留下深刻印象、對……感到讚賞或欽佩」。
examples: The manager was highly impressed with his interview performance.
I wasn't very impressed with the hotel's customer service.

grammarNotes.title: As... as... comparison
grammarNotes.explanation: 「not as + 形容詞 + as」結構，表示「不像……那樣……」。這裡指大人被驚豔的程度，不如法蘭克想像中「應該要有的」那麼多。
examples: The exam was not as difficult as I thought it would be.
This project isn't taking as much time as we expected.

sentences.englishOriginal: “You’ll have wrinkles one day, too,” Mom said, rubbing on some lotion.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「總有一天你也會長皺紋的，」媽媽一邊往臉上塗抹乳液一邊說。

phraseNotes.title: one day
phraseNotes.explanation: 在描述未來時，表示「總有一天、遲早」；如果是描述過去，則表示「某一天」。
examples: I hope to travel around the world one day.
One day, he just packed his bags and left without a word.

sentences.englishOriginal: “Some things are better left unsaid,” said Mr. Wiggins, adjusting his toupee.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「有些事最好還是看破不說破，」威金斯先生邊調整他的假髮邊說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: better left unsaid
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 極為道地的英文成語、固定表達法。意思是「有些事情不說出來反而比較好」、「保持沉默才是上策」。
examples: It was an awkward situation, so I decided some things are better left unsaid.
He wanted to complain, but realized his opinion was better left unsaid.

sentences.englishOriginal: “You don’t have to say everything you’re thinking,” said Ms. Zaroma, popping a mint in her mouth.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「你不需要把心裡想的每一件事都說出來，」薩羅馬小姐邊說邊往嘴裡丟了一顆薄荷糖。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: popping ... in one's mouth
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 動詞「pop」在這裡是用作快速、輕巧地「把某物塞進/丟進嘴裡」的生動表達法，比單純用 eat 或 put 更具畫面感。
examples: She was popping grapes into her mouth while watching TV.
He popped a piece of chewing gum in his mouth before the meeting.

sentences.englishOriginal: Frank’s schoolmates weren’t impressed, either.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 法蘭克的同學們也同樣不買單。

sentences.englishOriginal: “My freckles do not look like the Big Dipper,” Dotty said. “And my mother says I shouldn’t listen to you.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我的雀斑才不像北斗七星哩，」多蒂說。「而且我媽媽說我不應該聽你瞎扯。」

sentences.englishOriginal: “I’m still going to sing in the talent show at the school carnival tomorrow – but I’m not going to ride the Ferris wheel with you,” Carol told Frank.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「明天的學校園遊會才藝表演我還是會去唱——但我才不要跟你一起搭摩天輪咧，」卡洛對法蘭克說。

vocabularyNotes.title: carnival
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「嘉年華會、園遊會、遊樂場」。在美國學校常指伴隨有簡單遊樂設施、攤位活動的慶典。
examples: The annual school carnival raises money for new library books.
We played games and ate cotton candy at the neighborhood carnival.

vocabularyNotes.title: Ferris wheel
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，遊樂園裡的「摩天輪」。注意第一個字通常會大寫。
examples: From the top of the Ferris wheel, you can see the whole city.
She felt a little dizzy when the Ferris wheel stopped at the very top.

sentences.englishOriginal: “But we always ride the Ferris wheel together,” Frank whispered.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「可是我們向來都是一起搭摩天輪的啊，」法蘭克低聲嘟囔著。

sentences.englishOriginal: Everyone was upset, and now Frank wasn’t so happy either.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 每個人都很不高興，而現在法蘭克自己心裡也堵得慌。

sentences.englishOriginal: Frank clipped his toenails and alphabetized his bug collection, but he didn’t feel any better, so he walked down the block to Grandpa’s house.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 法蘭克修剪了腳趾甲，還把他的昆蟲收藏按字母順序排列，但他心情並沒有好過一點，於是他就順著街區走到爺爺家。

vocabularyNotes.title: alphabetize
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「按字母順序排列」。
examples: The librarian is alphabetizing the new books on the shelf.
Please alphabetize the student files by their last names.

sentences.englishOriginal: Grandpa Ernest was on the porch, clipping his toenails.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 歐內斯特爺爺當時正在門廊上剪著腳趾甲。

vocabularyNotes.title: porch
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指西式房屋延伸出去的有頂「門廊、陽台」。
examples: We sat on the front porch and drank iced tea all afternoon.
There is a comfortable swing on their back porch.

sentences.englishOriginal: “Incoming!” he yelled.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「砲彈來襲！」他大喊道。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Incoming!
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 軍事或遊戲常用語，指「敵襲、有東西飛過來了！」。這裡爺爺剪腳趾甲時大喊這句，是在幽默地警告法蘭克小心被彈飛的趾甲砸到。
examples: Incoming! Duck under the table!
He heard someone shout 'Incoming!' just before the ball hit the fence.

sentences.englishOriginal: Frank ducked.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 法蘭克趕緊迅速蹲下低頭。

vocabularyNotes.title: duck
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞是鴨子，但在此處作為動詞，意為「迅速低頭（以躲避攻擊或障礙物）」或「猛然蹲下」。
examples: You need to duck your head when walking through that low doorway.
He ducked behind the counter so nobody would see him.

sentences.englishOriginal: “What’s up, Frank?” Grandpa said.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「怎麼啦，法蘭克？」爺爺問道。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: What's up?
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 極為普遍的道地美式問候語，相當於「最近怎麼樣？」或「發生什麼事了？」。
examples: Hey man, what's up? I haven't seen you in weeks.
You look worried. What's up?

sentences.englishOriginal: “Everyone is mad at me for being frank.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「每個人都在氣我說話太直了。」

phraseNotes.title: mad at someone for something
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用口語片語，表示「因為某事而對某人生氣」。
examples: Are you still mad at me for forgetting your birthday?
She was mad at her brother for breaking her laptop.

sentences.englishOriginal: I’m not sure honesty is the best policy anymore.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我現在不太確定誠實是不是最好的處事方針了。」

sentences.englishOriginal: “That used to happen to me, too,” said Grandpa.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我以前也常常遇到這種事，」爺爺說。

phraseNotes.title: used to
phraseNotes.explanation: 助動詞片語，表示「過去習慣、過去常常（但現在已經不這樣了）」，後面必須接原形動詞。
examples: I used to swim every morning when I lived near the beach.
This building used to be a cinema many years ago.

sentences.englishOriginal: Mrs. Peacock walked by and straightened her massive hat.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 皮考克太太正好路過，順手扶正了她那頂巨大的帽子。

vocabularyNotes.title: massive
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，形容體積「巨大的、龐大的」。
examples: The house has a massive stone fireplace in the living room.
They faced a massive challenge but refused to give up.

phraseNotes.title: walk by
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意為「走過、路過」。
examples: A stranger walked by and gave me a warm smile.
I see her walk by my office window every morning.

sentences.englishOriginal: “It’s new! Do you like it, Ernest?”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這是新買的！你喜歡嗎，歐內斯特？」

sentences.englishOriginal: Frank thought the hat looked like a flower store on Mrs. Peacock’s head.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 法蘭克覺得那頂帽子戴在皮考克太太頭上，簡直就像是一家花店。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Well,” Grandpa said, “there are an awful lot of flowers up there.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「嗯，」爺爺說，「那上面的花可真是有夠多。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: an awful lot of
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 非常地道的美式口語表達，用來強調數量「非常多、極多」，語氣比許多教科書教的 lots of 或 a lot of 更為強烈。
examples: It took an awful lot of work to clean up the garage.
He spent an awful lot of money on his new hobby.

sentences.englishOriginal: But my favorite is the purple one in the middle.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 不過我最喜歡的是中間那一朵紫色的。」

sentences.englishOriginal: “Thank you!” Mrs. Peacock beamed. “Toodle-oo!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「謝謝你！」皮考克太太喜笑顏開。「再會啦！」

vocabularyNotes.title: beam
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，除了指光線照射，常用來形容人「眉開眼笑、欣喜地微笑」。
examples: The proud mother beamed as her son accepted the award.
She beamed with joy when she heard the good news.

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Toodle-oo!
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 一種略帶俏皮、老派且英式的日常道別語，相當於「拜拜、回見」。常出現在比較幽默或具有特別優雅/古怪性格的角色口中。
examples: Right then, I'm off to the shops. Toodle-oo!
She waved her hand and shouted 'Toodle-oo!' as she stepped onto the bus.

sentences.englishOriginal: Frank couldn’t believe his ears.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 法蘭克簡直不敢相信自己的耳朵。

phraseNotes.title: cannot believe one's ears
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用生動諺語，形容聽到某些令人極度震驚、難以置信的消息時「不敢相信自己的耳朵」。
examples: When they announced my name as the winner, I couldn't believe my ears.
She couldn't believe her ears when he told her the secret.

sentences.englishOriginal: “Grandpa, you lied!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「爺爺，你撒謊！」

sentences.englishOriginal: “No, Frank, I told the truth. I really do like the purple flower best.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「不，法蘭克，我說的是實話。我確實最喜歡那朵紫色的花。」

grammarNotes.title: Emphatic 'do/does/did'
grammarNotes.explanation: 在肯定句的動詞前面加上助動詞 do/does/did，用來「加強語氣」，相當於中文的「真的、確實」。這裡爺爺為了向法蘭克澄清他沒有說謊，特別加強語氣說他「確實」最喜歡紫色那一朵。
examples: I do believe that honesty matters.
She does look nice in that green dress.

sentences.englishOriginal: I talked about the good things on her hat instead of the not-so-good things.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我只是挑了她帽子上好看的部分來說，而不是去評論那些不太好看的地方。」

phraseNotes.title: instead of
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意為「而不是……、代替……」，後面常接名詞或動名詞。
examples: Let's walk to the restaurant instead of taking a taxi.
He ordered a salad instead of a burger for lunch.

sentences.englishOriginal: Grandpa’s best friend, Mr. Pickles, pulled up in his truck.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 爺爺最好的朋友皮克司先生開著卡車停了下來。

phraseNotes.title: pull up
phraseNotes.explanation: 特指駕駛車輛時「開過去並停下來」的動作。
examples: A police car pulled up outside our house last night.
The bus pulled up at the curb to let passengers off.

sentences.englishOriginal: “Try this relish,” he called, handing Grandpa a jar and a spoon. “I’ve got a cold and can’t taste a thing.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「嚐嚐這個酸黃瓜醬，」他喊著，順手遞給爺爺一個罐子和湯匙。「我感冒了，什麼味道都嚐不出來。」

vocabularyNotes.title: relish
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，一種西式常用的「調味醬、酸菜醬」（通常由切碎的酸黃瓜、蔬菜和香料製成，常用於熱狗或漢堡）。
examples: Would you like ketchup or relish on your hot dog?
He makes his own sweet relish from fresh cucumbers.

nativeExpressionNotes.title: can't taste a thing
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 道地的口語說法，比單純說 cannot taste anything 更有張力，用來強調「完全嚐不出任何一點味道」。
examples: My nose is blocked, so I can't taste a thing.
The soup was so hot it burnt my tongue, and now I can't taste a thing.

sentences.englishOriginal: Grandpa scooped a spoonful of relish into his mouth.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 爺爺挖了一大匙酸黃瓜醬放進嘴裡。

vocabularyNotes.title: scoop
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指用湯匙或勺子「舀取、挖取」。
examples: She scooped two balls of chocolate ice cream into a bowl.
He scooped up some sand to inspect the shells.

sentences.englishOriginal: His nose twitched. His eyes watered. His hair stood on end.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他的鼻子抽動了幾下。他的眼淚流了出來。他的頭髮全都豎了起來。

vocabularyNotes.title: twitch
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指肌肉或身體部位「抽動、痙攣」。
examples: His left eyelid started to twitch from lack of sleep.
The rabbit's nose twitched as it sniffed the air.

nativeExpressionNotes.title: hair stood on end
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 慣用語，字面意思是頭髮直立，可用來形容人「感到極度恐懼、震驚」或在這裡指被極端強烈的味道（過辣或過酸）給「震撼到全身發麻」。
examples: The ghost story made my hair stand on end.
When the lightning struck nearby, everyone's hair stood on end.

sentences.englishOriginal: “Give it to me straight, Ernest,” said Mr. Pickles. “I can’t sell it if it’s no good.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「老實跟我開門見山地說吧，歐內斯特，」皮克司先生說。「如果這東西不好，我是沒法賣的。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Give it to me straight
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 經典道地的口語。意思是「有話直說、別拐彎抹角、坦白告訴我實情」。
examples: Don't try to spare my feelings, just give it to me straight.
The doctor gave it to him straight about his condition.

sentences.englishOriginal: Grandpa wiped his eyes. “More… sugar… less… pepper!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 爺爺擦了擦眼淚。「糖要多放點……胡椒……少放點！」

sentences.englishOriginal: Mr. Pickles grinned and shook Grandpa’s hand. “Back to the drawing board! Thanks for your help!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 皮克司先生咧嘴一笑，跟爺爺握了握手。「看來得重頭來過囉！謝啦！」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Back to the drawing board
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 非常常見的成語。字面意思是回到繪圖板前，指某個計畫或產品宣告失敗，必須「推翻既有架構，全部重新開始設計」。
examples: Our marketing plan didn't work at all, so it's back to the drawing board.
The prototype failed the safety test, sending the engineers back to the drawing board.

sentences.englishOriginal: “Grandpa, he didn’t get mad at you!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「爺爺，他居然沒有對你發脾氣！」

sentences.englishOriginal: “That’s right.” Grandpa drank some water. “He asked me for the truth… and I gave it to him.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「沒錯。」爺爺喝了點水。「是他主動問我實話的……所以我就如實相告了。」

sentences.englishOriginal: Frank sighed. “So I shouldn’t give the truth unless someone asks for it?”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 法蘭克嘆了口氣。「所以除非別人主動問起，否則我不應該隨便把實話說出來囉？」

grammarNotes.title: Unless (Negative Condition)
grammarNotes.explanation: 連接詞 unless 意為「除非……否則不……」，相當於 if... not...，用來引導一個否定條件。
examples: You cannot gain access to the building unless you have an ID card.
I won't go to the party unless you come with me.

sentences.englishOriginal: “You should always give people the truth,” Grandpa said. “You just have to find the right way to serve it.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「你應該永遠對人保持誠實，」爺爺說。「你只是得找到正確的方法把它『端上桌』。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: serve the truth
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 爺爺在這裡用了一個妙喻。一般「serve」是指餐廳把佳餚「端上桌、服務」，這裡用來比喻包裝實話的「表達方式」，呼應前面提到的糖跟胡椒。
examples: Telling a harsh truth is easy; knowing how to serve it with kindness is an art.

sentences.englishOriginal: The next afternoon at the school carnival, when Ms. Zaroma laughed like a horse, Frank said, “You tell great jokes.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 隔天下午在學校園遊會上，當薩羅馬小姐笑得像隻馬一樣時，法蘭克說：「妳真的很會講笑話。」

sentences.englishOriginal: When Mr. Wiggins did a weird dance, Frank said, “Impressive spins, sir!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當威金斯校長跳起奇怪的舞時，法蘭克說：「長官，您的旋轉動作真是令人驚艷！」

sentences.englishOriginal: When Frank saw Dotty, he said “I like dots better than squares.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當法蘭克看到多蒂時，他說：「比起正方形，我更喜歡圓點點。」

phraseNotes.title: like ... better than ...
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用比較級片語，等同於 prefer ... to ...，意為「比起後者，更喜歡前者」。
examples: I like winter better than summer.
She likes reading books better than watching movies.

sentences.englishOriginal: And when Carol sang, Frank said, “You sure can hit those high notes.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而當卡洛唱歌時，法蘭克說：「妳確實非常能飆出那些高音呢。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: hit high notes
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 音樂歌唱道地口語，指「唱出高音、飆高音」。
examples: She practice hard every day to be able to hit those high notes safely.
The crowd cheered when the singer hit the perfect high note.

sentences.englishOriginal: Carol smiled at Frank. “Dotty and I are going to ride the Ferris wheel. Want to come?”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 卡洛對著法蘭克笑了笑。「多蒂跟我正準備去搭摩天輪。你要一起來嗎？」

sentences.englishOriginal: On the way home, Frank gave his mom a poem he wrote for her.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在回家的路上，法蘭克給了他媽媽一首他為她寫的詩。

sentences.englishOriginal: Mom, your hair looks nice today. I only see a little gray.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 媽媽，妳今天的頭髮看起來真好看。我只看到一點點白頭髮而已。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: gray (hair)
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 在英文中，長輩年長冒出的「白頭髮」，母語者通常會使用 gray hair（灰色頭髮）而不是 white hair。
examples: He started to get gray hair at the temples when he turned forty.
She dyes her hair to cover up the gray.

sentences.englishOriginal: And when she smiled, Frank decided not to mention the wrinkles around the corners of her mouth.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當她露出微笑時，法蘭克決定體貼地不提及她嘴角周圍的那些皺紋。

vocabularyNotes.title: mention
examples: He didn't mention the problem during the budget meeting.
Did she happen to mention when she would be arriving?

sentences.englishOriginal: The next day, while Frank helped Grandpa in his garden, Mrs. Peacock stopped by again.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 隔天，當法蘭克在花園裡幫忙爺爺時，皮考克太太又順道過來了。

phraseNotes.title: stop by
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語片語，意為「順道拜訪、中途短暫停留」。
examples: I'll stop by your house this evening to drop off the book.
Can you stop by the bakery and pick up some bread?

sentences.englishOriginal: “You were right!” she said to Grandpa. “That other hat had too many flowers.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「你說的沒錯！」她對爺爺說。「之前那頂帽子的花實在是太多了。」

sentences.englishOriginal: Just then, Mr. Pickles arrived with a new jar of relish for Grandpa to try.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 就在那時，皮克司先生帶著一罐全新改良的酸黃瓜醬走來，要讓爺爺品嚐。

phraseNotes.title: just then
phraseNotes.explanation: 時間副詞片語，常用在故事敘述中，表示「就在那個時候、正當那時」。
examples: I was about to leave the house. Just then, the phone rang.
Just then, the lights went out, and the room became pitch black.

sentences.englishOriginal: “Excuse me a moment,” Grandpa said to Mrs. Peacock.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「請失陪一下，」爺爺對皮考克太太說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “So, what do you think of my hat?” Mrs. Peacock asked. But this time, she wasn’t asking Grandpa. She was asking Frank.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「那麼，你覺得我的帽子怎麼樣？」皮考克太太問。但這一次，她問的不是爺爺，而是轉過頭來問法蘭克。

phraseNotes.title: think of
phraseNotes.explanation: 當用作「What do you think of...?」時，用來詢問對方對某人或某事的「看法或評價」。
examples: What do you think of the new design?
I don't know much about art, but what do you think of this painting?

sentences.englishOriginal: Frank looked at Grandpa on the porch with Mr. Pickles. Grandpa was taking another spoonful of relish, and he wasn’t drinking any water. Frank knew just what to say.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 法蘭克看著陽台上正與皮克司先生待在一起的爺爺。爺爺正吃著另一口酸黃瓜醬，而且這次他完全不需要喝水。法蘭克頓時明白該怎麼說了。

sentences.englishOriginal: “I’ve never seen a hat like that before. It must be one of a kind.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我以前從來沒有見過像這樣的帽子。這一定是最獨一無二的。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: one of a kind
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 極為地道且高雅的形容詞片語。意為「獨一無二的、世上少有的、絕無僅有的」。法蘭克用這個詞極其巧妙且誠實地讚美了帽子，既不違心，又讓對方非常高興。
examples: This antique watch is truly one of a kind.
She has a very personal style; she is one of a kind.

sentences.englishOriginal: “Thank you!” Mrs. Peacock beamed. “Toodle-oo!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「謝謝你！」皮考克太太笑得合不攏嘴。「再會啦！」

sentences.englishOriginal: “So how is it being Frank today?” Grandpa asked.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「那麼，今天做一個『心直口快』的法蘭克感覺如何呀？」爺爺笑著問。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Well, Grandpa, I still think honesty is the best policy,” said Frank, “but now I know it’s best served with more sugar… …and less pepper!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「這個嘛，爺爺，我依然覺得誠實是最好的方針，」法蘭克說，「但現在我明白，實話最好能配上多一點的糖……還有少一點的胡椒！」

sentences.englishOriginal: The end.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 全劇終。

sentences.englishOriginal: I was given this book to read, and I read it and I loved it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當時我拿到了這本書來朗讀，我看過之後就深深愛上了它。

grammarNotes.title: Passive Voice with Double Objects
grammarNotes.explanation: 由雙賓語（give someone something）演變而來的被動語態「I was given this book」，意為「我被給予了這本書/我拿到了這本書」。這比主動語態「Someone gave me this book」更常被英語母語者用來強調主角目前的狀態。
examples: He was given a second chance by the manager.
We were given detailed instructions before the exam.

sentences.englishOriginal: I loved the message in it, because, I, Bob Odenkirk, have often prided myself on being honest and frank, when in truth you can be a little rude or you can hurt people’s feelings, so, you have to decide how and the way you’re gonna be honest.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我很喜歡這本書傳達的寓意。因為，我鮑勃·奧登科克本人，也常常因為自己的誠實和直率而感到自豪；但事實上，有時你可能會顯得有點無禮，或者不小心傷害了他人的感受。所以，你必須拿捏自己該如何以及用什麼樣的方式去保持誠實。

phraseNotes.title: pride oneself on something
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「以某事引以為傲、引以為豪」。
examples: The hotel prides itself on its high-quality service.
She prided herself on her punctuality.

nativeExpressionNotes.title: when in truth
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 轉折句型，意思是「但事實上、但實際上卻是……」，常用來引出一個與前半句理想狀態相反的殘酷現實。
examples: He pretended to be happy, when in truth he was feeling incredibly lonely.

sentences.englishOriginal: So, anyway, the message in this book was one that I liked very much ‘cause I very much need this lesson for myself.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 總之，這本書所傳達的啟示是我非常喜歡的，因為我自己也非常需要好好學習這一課。

sentences.englishOriginal: Thank you for watching Storyline Online. Make sure to check out all our stories. Keep watching, and keep reading.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 感謝收看 Storyline Online。請務必去看看我們所有的故事。保持觀看，並保持閱讀習慣。

phraseNotes.title: make sure to
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意為「務必、確保去（做某事）」。
examples: Make sure to lock the door before you leave.
Please make sure to check your spelling in the essay.

phraseNotes.title: check out
phraseNotes.explanation: 多義片語，在日常口語或網路用語中，常用來表示「去看看、去瞧瞧（某個新奇或推薦的事物）」。
examples: You should check out that new cafe down the street.
Check out this video; it's hilarious!
